items.title: Fast volumetric fluorescence lifetime imaging of multicellular systems using single-objective light-sheet microscopy
items.author: Pierre-François Lenne
items.content: The authors introduce fluorescence lifetime microscopy using a novel SPAD array detector in a single-objective light-sheet microscope. This imaging system achieves 10–100-fold improvement in imaging speed compared to a confocal fluorescence lifetime imaging microscope.

items.title: Macrophage ITGAV is dispensable for post-infarction remodeling in mice and does not mediate fibronectin responses
items.author: Nikolaos G. Frangogiannis
items.content: Macrophage ITGAV has minimal impact on repair, remodeling and fibrosis after myocardial infarction, reflecting the limited role of ITGAV in mediating fibronectin-dependent signaling in macrophages.

items.title: SNX16 aggravates AngII-induced cardiac hypertrophy in mice via EGFR transactivation
items.author: Ke-Yu Deng
items.content: SN16X is upregulated in mouse hypertrophic hearts and accelerates AngII-induced cardiac hypertrophy via enhancing EGFR recycling and transactivation.

items.title: Spinach (Spinacia oleracea) has epidermal bladder cells and exhibits characteristics of a facultative halophyte
items.author: Maria Betânia G. S. Freire
items.content: Spinach has epidermal bladder cells that accumulate Cl and K, a feature not previously documented in this species. Like quinoa, spinach maintains growth under salinity, suggesting facultative halophyte status and unique ion partitioning.

items.title: N-palmitoyl glycine differentially modulates TRPM4 and TRPC5 and is causally linked to Brugada syndrome
items.author: Lin Wu
items.content: Using mendelian randomization and patch-clamp experiments in cardiomyocytes, N-palmitoyl glycine is shown to contribute to the pathophysiology of Brugada syndrome through the channel proteins TRPM4 and TRPC5.

items.title: Lactate orchestrates the TGFβ pathway and ferroptosis nexus in organ fibrosis via USP2 lactylation
items.author: Zhen Liang
items.content: TGFβ1 and ferroptosis drive organ fibrosis, with unclear crosstalk. USP2 deubiquitinates TXNDC5 (activating TGFβ signalling) and TFRC (promoting ferroptosis). TGFβ1 lactylates USP2 to boost its activity, highlighting USP2 as a therapeutic target

items.title: Shaping 3D minimal model tissues with mechanical constraints to orchestrate muscle differentiation
items.author: Myriam Reffay
items.content: Mechanical forces and topology drive self-organization of C2C12 myoblasts into 3D nematic tissues. Coupling a tissue-magnetic stretcher with RNA-FISH imaging reveals that alignment and stress guide muscle differentiation at ordered regions.

items.title: Publisher Correction: Activation energies of both constructive and destructive cellular biochemistry determine maximum growth temperature in archaea
items.author: François Guyot
items.content: Publisher Correction: Activation energies of both constructive and destructive cellular biochemistry determine maximum growth temperature in archaea
